heroHeadline: Sheinbaum at 561 Days: Barcelona GPM Summit, USMCA Round 2, Universal Healthcare Launch, 41% Homicide Drop
heroSubtitle: President Sheinbaum traveled to Barcelona on April 18 for the Global Progressive Mobilisation (GPM) summit, her first official international trip — joining Lula, Petro, Orsi, and Spanish PM Sánchez to coordinate a multilateral progressive response to rising global authoritarianism and formally restoring Mexico-Spain diplomatic relations after an eight-year estrangement. The Universal Health Credential registration opened April 13 for 120 million Mexicans. A second round of USMCA technical talks is scheduled for April 20 in Mexico City. Sheinbaum announced a 41% reduction in intentional homicides vs. December 2024, with March 2026 the lowest since 2015. The Senate approved a national femicide reform, and Citlalli Hernández resigned from the Secretariat of Women to prepare Morena for 2027 midterms. A Pemex oil spill at the Abkatun offshore platform soiled 48 beaches. Time Magazine named Sheinbaum to its 100 Most Influential People list for the second straight year.
footerNote: Data sourced from Diario Oficial, INEGI, Banco de México, SNSP, Secretaría de Economía, Reuters, AP, El Universal, Reforma, Animal Político, Mexico News Daily, Infobae, Milenio, La Jornada, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, AtlasIntel, USTR, Chatham House, Mexico Business News, El Financiero, La Silla Rota, Proceso, CNN Español, Lopez Doriga, The Yucatan Times, Excelsior, La Razón de México, Quinto Poder
